---
title: "between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 8, 18, 17, 17, 26]  # aa-col-added; raw-p added
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-codon log2 fold-change of day_5-vs-day_0 median per-replicate frequency, with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint, across the 61 sense codons. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched at day_5 (log2_FC >= 0), red = depleted at day_5 (log2_FC < 0). Y-axis range ~-1.35 to +1.40 across panels.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/codon/composite/APE_codon_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 61 sense codons by signed log2_FC (day_5 median / day_0 median) at one ribosome site. Bars above zero are day_5-enriched relative to day_0; bars below zero are day_5-depleted.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=4 day_5 (BWM_d5_rep2/3 + control_d5_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_d0_rep2/3 + control_d0_rep2/3); BWM and control reps pooled inside each timepoint; two-sided MW on per-replicate codon frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 61 sense codons); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_d5 / median_d0).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor. Empirically: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in any of the 6 files.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control reps; if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 MW has very low power; no-signal results across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 (0/183) for codon-level day_5 vs day_0 MW with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint (n=4 per side); min raw p = 0.0286 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor). Min p_adj = 0.436 at site P — 4 floor rows (CTA -0.923, GCG -1.212, GCA -0.740, GAG +0.190), giving BH wall 61 x 0.0286 / 4 = 0.436. File-wide: 5 floor rows (1 at A: ACT +0.227; 4 at P; 0 at E). Site A min p_adj = 0.775; site E completely flat (min p_adj = 0.926). Closest-to-significant codon-level signature: at site P, three depletions (CTA, GCG, GCA) and one enrichment (GAG) all reaching the floor between day_0 and day_5 reps. Treat as exploratory; the floor blocks formal FDR<0.05 here regardless of biology.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (day_5 / day_0 median ratio). `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected per A/P/E site (family of 61 codon tests). Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means day_5-enriched; negative means day_5-depleted. Within each sub-table, rows are up to 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker. The `low-count` flag is applied when `min(median_day_5, median_day_0) < 0.005` (verifiable via `python scripts/dylan_table_checker.py <CSV> --family wilcoxon`). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag  |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----- |
| ACT   | T   | +0.227             | 0.0286            | 0.775                | floor |
| AAC   | N   | +0.672             | 0.1143            | 0.775                |       |
| ATC   | I   | +0.470             | 0.2000            | 0.775                |       |
| TCT   | S   | +0.250             | 0.2000            | 0.775                |       |
| CAA   | Q   | +0.158             | 0.2000            | 0.775                |       |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| TTA   | L   | -1.341             | 0.1143            | 0.775                | low-count |
| GCA   | A   | -0.713             | 0.1143            | 0.775                |           |
| GGC   | G   | -0.683             | 0.1143            | 0.775                | low-count |
| CTG   | L   | -0.652             | 0.1143            | 0.775                | low-count |
| TGG   | W   | -0.671             | 0.2000            | 0.775                |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| GAG   | E   | +0.190             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor     |
| GGG   | G   | +1.001             | 0.1143            | 0.775                | low-count |
| TCT   | S   | +0.388             | 0.1143            | 0.775                |           |
| TCC   | S   | +0.444             | 0.2000            | 0.871                |           |
| GTC   | V   | +0.335             | 0.2000            | 0.871                |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag             |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ---------------- |
| GCG   | A   | -1.212             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor, low-count |
| CTA   | L   | -0.923             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor, low-count |
| GCA   | A   | -0.740             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor, low-count |
| GGT   | G   | -0.115             | 0.0571            | 0.697                |                  |
| AGG   | R   | -0.729             | 0.1143            | 0.775                | low-count        |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag  |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----- |
| TCC   | S   | +0.442             | 0.0571            | 0.926                |       |
| CAA   | Q   | +0.433             | 0.2000            | 0.926                |       |
| TCT   | S   | +0.385             | 0.2000            | 0.926                |       |
| CAG   | Q   | +0.312             | 0.2000            | 0.926                |       |
| ACT   | T   | +0.199             | 0.2000            | 0.926                |       |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| CCA   | P   | -0.525             | 0.1143            | 0.926                |           |
| ACC   | T   | -0.143             | 0.1143            | 0.926                |           |
| GCG   | A   | -0.610             | 0.2000            | 0.926                | low-count |
| CTA   | L   | -0.398             | 0.2000            | 0.926                | low-count |
| AGA   | R   | -0.270             | 0.2000            | 0.926                |           |

### Flag glossary

- `floor` — the row sits at the test's discrete minimum possible raw p-value, given the sample size. For MW two-sided at n=4 vs n=4 this floor is exactly 0.02857. In a 61-codon per-site BH family, one floor hit BH-corrects to ~= 1.74 (cannot reach `p_adj` < 1 on its own); the P-site's observed `p_adj` = 0.436 arises only because 3 floor hits (and 1 enriched floor row) tie at the same site, giving 61 x 0.0286 / 4 = 0.436.
- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. At n=4 vs n=4 the smallest attainable raw p is the floor itself (0.02857), so the only raw p < 0.05 rows are floor rows; they carry `floor`, not `nominal-only`. No row in this file qualifies for `nominal-only` (the next discrete raw p above the floor is 0.0571, which is already above 0.05).
- `low-count` — `min(median_day_5, median_day_0) < 0.005`; the `log2_FC` column for this row is dominated by single-rep fluctuations and should not be over-read (verifiable via `python scripts/dylan_table_checker.py <CSV> --family wilcoxon`). Rare codons in this file's top |effect| set include TTA, GCG, CTA, GGG, GGC, CTG, AGG — all with median frequencies < 0.2%.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 183
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.436 (P-site, BH wall for 4 floor rows in 61-codon family)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02857 (= MW exact floor); 5 rows at the floor — A: ACT; P: CTA, GAG, GCA, GCG
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 two-sided exact)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.775 (1 floor row)
  - P site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.436 (4 floor rows)
  - E site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.926 (0 floor rows)

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across 183 codon-level day_5-vs-day_0 comparisons; site P breaks below the 0.5 BH wall with 4 floor rows tied at `p_adj` = 0.436 (concrete).** Raw-p is clamped at 0.02857 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor) and the 61-codon per-site BH family puts the wall for a lone floor hit at ~= 1.74 — i.e. no single floor row can reach `p_adj` < 1 alone. The site-P configuration here (4 floor rows: CTA, GCG, GCA all depleted; GAG enriched) is the only site in this file to break below the 0.5 BH ceiling. The `larger-bh-family` caveat records the matched aa-resolution d5-vs-d0 sister reaching `p_adj` = 0.571 with 1 floor hit; codon resolution lives further from FDR<0.05 in this file (`p_adj` = 0.436 with 4 floor hits) despite finer dynamic range, because the codon BH family is ~3x larger.

**Site-P codon hits concentrate on Ala and Leu synonyms plus one enriched Glu codon (exploratory).** The 4 site-P floor rows split as: two Ala codons depleted (GCA -0.740, GCG -1.212), one Leu codon depleted (CTA -0.923), and one Glu codon enriched (GAG +0.190). At codon resolution this is a synonym-specific signature on Ala and Leu rather than a whole-amino-acid shift; the `larger-bh-family` caveat compares to the aa-resolution sister reaching `p_adj` = 0.571 with only 1 floor hit, consistent with aa-aggregation averaging across synonyms (worth carrying to the aa-resolution sister and the within-condition codon Fisher; exploratory only, not a hit).

**Site A's lone floor row is ACT (+0.227, Thr), well below the magnitudes of the panel's non-floor extreme cells (exploratory).** The largest |effect| cells at A — TTA -1.341 (`low-count` Leu rare codon), AAC +0.672 (Asn), ATC +0.470 (Ile) — sit above ACT in magnitude but only ACT cleared the n=4 MW floor. This is the discrete-floor pattern at work: |log2_FC| magnitude and raw-p rank are partially decoupled at this design's exact-p floor. AAC (+0.672) is the most stable A-site exploratory cell (Asn is a moderate-frequency amino acid; no `low-count` flag here) — worth carrying to `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon` to check whether BWM and control individually corroborate (exploratory only, not a hit).

**Site E is flat at codon resolution for this contrast (concrete).** Min `p_adj` at E is 0.926 here with 0 floor rows file-wide for site E (Numbers-at-a-glance). Whatever timepoint shifts exist in d5-vs-d0 codon frequencies at stall windows concentrate at the A and P sites and bypass the E site — biologically consistent with E being the exit site, where the codon has already been decoded and the tRNA is on its way out of the ribosome.

**Most large |log2_FC| cells annotated `low-count` sit on rare codons; their magnitudes are not stable (exploratory).** The `low-count-rare-codon-instability` caveat names A-TTA (-1.341), P-GCG (-1.212), P-GGG (+1.001), P-CTA (-0.923) as cells with median freq < 0.2%; the P-Depleted table additionally flags GGT (no `low-count`, kept as the only non-low-count comparator) and AGG (-0.729, `low-count`), and the individual-plot captions annotate E-ATA (~+1.40) and E-TTA `low-count` on the E panel tail. Read |log2_FC| > 0.7 on these `low-count`-annotated cells as sampling-noise magnitudes, not biology. The two large-effect cells at A and P without a `low-count` annotation in this file are AAC (+0.672 at A, moderate-frequency Asn) and TGG (-0.671 at A, low-frequency Trp territory but no flag) — those are the cells worth carrying to the more-powered Fisher sister for replication (exploratory only, not hits).

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claims to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_5-vs-day_0 at codon resolution with conditions pooled; site P has 4 tied floor rows (CTA, GCG, GCA depleted; GAG enriched) hitting BH wall 0.436, the only site in this file to break below the 0.5 BH ceiling; site E is flat at codon resolution for this contrast (min `p_adj` = 0.926, 0 floor rows file-wide). Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the site-P 4-codon floor configuration (Ala synonyms GCA/GCG depleted, Leu codon CTA depleted, Glu codon GAG enriched) reproduce in `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon` and `per_timepoint_fisher_codon`? (2) does A-AAC's enrichment (+0.672, no `low-count` flag here) clear FDR in the more-powered Fisher contrasts? (3) does P-GAG's enrichment (+0.190, floor here) reach FDR in the within-condition Fisher? Noise to ignore: every `low-count`-annotated cell with |log2_FC| > 0.7 (TTA, GCG, GGG, CTA, AGG, CTG, GGC) — their magnitudes are not biology-quotable numbers.

## Methods

MW rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate frequencies, n=4 day_5 (BWM_day5_rep2/3 + control_day5_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0, BH-FDR per site (each site = 61-codon family). Test answers "do day_5 and day_0 reps differ in per-rep codon frequency at this site?" with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** Raw-p is clamped at 0.0286 (n=4 vs n=4 floor); for a 61-codon per-site BH family, even 4 tied floor hits at one site only reach `p_adj` = 0.436. "0/183 at FDR<0.05" here is the design's structural ceiling, not biological evidence that day_5 ~= day_0.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each timepoint's n=4 is 2 BWM + 2 control reps. The site-P 4-floor block here cannot be attributed to BWM or control individually — use `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon` to disentangle.
- **n=4-low-power.** Low power; the no-signal result is weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** Headline above explicitly flags 0.0286 as the raw-p floor and the structural source of "no FDR hits".
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` of medians only; rare codons (TTA, CTA, GCG, GGG all < 0.2% median freq) get the same column real estate as common codons and dominate the |effect| tails.
- **larger-bh-family.** 61 codons per site -> BH wall ~3x more stringent than aa. The aa-level d5_vs_d0 sister gets to `p_adj` = 0.571 with 1 floor hit; here at codon level, 4 floor rows still only reach `p_adj` = 0.436. Codon resolution lives further from FDR<0.05 than aa resolution despite finer dynamic range.
- **low-count-rare-codon-instability.** Extreme |effect| at A-TTA (-1.341), P-GCG (-1.212), P-GGG (+1.001), P-CTA (-0.923) all sit on rare codons (median freq < 0.2%). Their magnitudes are not stable across small sample fluctuations.

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy may default to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied zero-frequency reps for rare codons, which would shift p-values away from the exact-floor bound — a higher risk here than for the n=6 between-condition file because the smaller n=4-per-arm sample and codon-resolution rare counts compound. **Ruled out, empirically verified** (see audit in `scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py`, invoked as `python scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py --design between_timepoint --timepoints day5 day0 --level codon`): only 2 of 183 (site, codon) tests have any rank ties in the pooled 8-element sample. For those 2, `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu(method='auto')` chose the **asymptotic** branch; for the remaining 181 it chose **exact**. Recomputed p-values match the pipeline CSV to ~8e-17, so the pipeline's reported p-values are already the right scipy auto-branch choice (181 exact + 2 asymptotic). As a counterfactual, forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all 183 tests shifts raw p-values by at most 0.124 (median 0.006), flips **zero** raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 either way, with min p_adj A/P/E ~= 0.71/0.46/0.90 vs the as-shipped exact 0.77/0.44/0.93. The branch choice does not affect any FDR-level conclusion in this file.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_5-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: AAC, AAT, ATC, GAT, CGC, CGG, GTC, TCT, ACT, TGT, ATT, CTA, GAC, CAA, GTT, ...; right: ... CCT, TTG, TCG, GCG, CGA, ATA, GTG, CTG, ACG, TGG, GGC, GCA, TTA). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.34 to +0.67. Largest enrichment at AAC (~+0.67); largest depletion at TTA (~-1.34, `low-count`). One floor row visible mid-pack at ACT (+0.227) — floor flag tracks raw-p, not effect size.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_A_codon_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![P-site day_5-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: GGG, TCC, TTT, TGT, TCT, GTC, AAC, CAA, GCT, GAG, ACT, GAA, GCC, CTC, ATC, ...; right: ... TGG, CAC, GGC, CTG, ATG, AGT, CCG, CGA, CCT, AGG, GCA, CTA, TTA, GCG). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.21 to +1.00. Largest enrichment at GGG (~+1.00, `low-count`); largest depletions at GCG (~-1.21, floor + `low-count`), TTA (~-1.00, `low-count`), CTA (~-0.92, floor + `low-count`), GCA (~-0.74, floor + `low-count`). 4 floor rows visible across both directions — site-P min p_adj = 0.436, the file's headline.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_P_codon_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![E-site day_5-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: ATA, ACG, TCG, TCC, CAA, TCT, GAG, CAG, TTG, TGT, ACT, CGG, GAA, TGC, CTC, ...; right: ... ATG, AGT, GTT, AGA, TAT, AAA, CTA, AGG, GGG, GCA, CCA, CGA, GCG, TTA, CCT). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.96 to +1.40. Largest enrichment at ATA (~+1.40, `low-count` Ile rare codon); largest depletions at CCT (~-0.96), TTA (~-0.91, `low-count`). No floor rows — site E completely flat at `p_adj` = 0.926 despite this panel having the widest displayed dynamic range of any plot in this file.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_E_codon_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)
